2606.26021.title: Privacy Vulnerabilities of Attention Layers in Tabular Foundation Models and Protection of High-Risk Queries
2606.26021.paper_summary: Tabular foundation models are commonly assumed to present limited privacy concerns as they are often pre-trained on large collections of synthetic data. However, these models leverage in-context learning, where sensitive records may be provided directly at inference time as labelled context examples. In this paper, we demonstrate that predictions generated via the attention mechanism leak sufficient information to enable effective Membership Inference Attacks (MIAs). To highlight this vulnerability, we propose AMIA (Attention-based Membership Inference Attack), a shadow-model-free attack that exploits the concentration of transformer attention patterns. Our results show that attention mechanisms reveal strong membership signals, which exceed classical confidence-based attacks, achieving an average gain of 7.7\%, specially in low false-positive regimes. To mitigate this risk, we introduce an inference-time defence inspired by $k$-anonymity principles. This approach reduces the uniqueness of context-key representations without introducing random noise or retraining the model. By targeting only high-risk queries identified through AMIA scores, the defence substantially reduces membership leakage of this attack by an average of 50\% and 25\% against confidence-based attacks, while preserving predictive utility with only 3.9\% performance degradation. Beyond showing that context examples are vulnerable, we further demonstrate that fine-tuning introduces an additional source of privacy risk. In particular, samples whose prediction confidence increases after fine-tuning become more susceptible to MIAs, indicating that fine-tuning can amplify memorisation and expose sensitive training information through confidence shifts.
2606.26021.paper_summary_zh: 表格基礎模型通常被認為在隱私方面的問題有限，因為它們經常在大量合成數據的集合上進行預訓練。<lb/>然而，這些模型利用上下文學習，其中敏感記錄可能在推斷時直接作為標記的上下文示例提供。<lb/>在本文中，我們展示了通過注意力機制生成的預測洩漏了足夠的信息，以使有效的成員推斷攻擊（MIAs）成為可能。<lb/>為了突出這一脆弱性，我們提出了AMIA（基於注意力的成員推斷攻擊），這是一種不依賴於影子模型的攻擊，利用了Transformer注意力模式的集中性。<lb/>我們的結果顯示，注意力機制揭示了強烈的成員信號，超過了經典的基於信心的攻擊，平均增益達到7.7\%，特別是在低假陽性範圍內。<lb/>為了減輕這一風險，我們引入了一種受$k$-匿名原則啟發的推斷時防禦方法。<lb/>這種方法在不引入隨機噪音或重新訓練模型的情況下，降低了上下文關鍵表示的唯一性。<lb/>通過僅針對通過AMIA分數識別的高風險查詢，該防禦方法將此攻擊的成員洩漏平均減少了50\%和25\%，同時保持預測效用，性能僅下降3.9\%。<lb/>除了顯示上下文示例是脆弱的外，我們還進一步展示了微調引入了額外的隱私風險。<lb/>特別是，經過微調後預測信心增加的樣本變得更容易受到MIAs的影響，這表明微調可以加強記憶並通過信心變化暴露敏感的訓練信息。
2606.26021.author: Tânia Carvalho et.al.
